target.name: Asimov
target.founder: Chris Voigt, Doug Densmore, Alec Nielsen, Raja Srinivas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $800M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asimov, a Series B biotech company, was valued at $800M post-money on $209M total raised. The valuation analysis triangulates across two applicable methodologies against a comparable universe of 3 public-sector peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Profluent.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $1.5B. Peer-median anchoring across same-sector, same-stage comparables produced $1.8B, with the three-company sample ranging from $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics) and a midpoint of $1.8B. Public-comp P/S anchoring was not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure. The triangulated range spans $1.3B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.7B across the two applied methods.

The current $800M valuation sits materially below both implied values, suggesting either a discount reflecting execution risk, fundraising conditions, or stage-specific dilution mechanics. Key uncertainties include the composition and maturity of the peer cohort—notably the wide $600M–$3.3B spread among direct comparables—and the absence of revenue-normalized valuation anchors. The $1.3B–$2.1B range should be stress-tested against disclosed financials and pipeline stage before concluding placement.
